svg.title: Livepeer OSS stack architecture
svg.desc: Layer diagram showing all major Livepeer repos organised by architectural role: on-chain protocol, core runtime, AI inference, real-time pipelines, transport, application SDKs, and developer tooling.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me more about livepeer/protocol — the smart contracts')

g.text: The node binary — every role runs from here

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me more about go-livepeer — what roles does it implement?')
g.text: Go · Orchestrator, Gateway, Transcoder, Broadcaster · calls livepeer/protocol contracts

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does ai-runner integrate with go-livepeer?')
g.text: Python · FastAPI · loads models, exposes REST

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is lpms and how does it relate to go-livepeer?')
sendPrompt('What is comfystream and how does it connect to the Livepeer network?')
sendPrompt('What is pytrickle and what is the trickle protocol?')

g.text: Consume the network from your app

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does livepeer-js cover — Studio API or AI API?')

g.text: Operator UX, identity, billing, creative apps

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is NaaP — Network as a Platform?')
sendPrompt('What is pymthouse — how does it handle billing and identity?')
sendPrompt('What is Storyboard — what does @livepeer/agent and @livepeer/creative-kit do?')
